title: Seeker and the Security Cats: The Potion Caper
summary: When Seeker, a magic researcher, wakes up to find his entire collection of completed potions stolen from his castle closet, he calls upon the trusty Security Cats—Wobbly Whiskers and Sarana—to solve the case. Following a trail of clues consisting of a dandelion flower and mysterious tiny dots in the dust, the detectives discover gray fur belonging to Silver, a mischievous cat owned by Isabella. With the help of Dimitar, the team boards Seeker's sky ship, flies over the Crystal Mountain, and travels to Roslindale in Boston. There, they find Silver playing in Isabella's sunroom. Silver confesses to taking the potions to test them, and she happily returns them from a bedroom closet. To make amends for taking them without asking, Silver gifts Seeker a special prehistoric land potion, teaching her the valuable lesson of asking before borrowing.

highlightQuote.text: But she can take flies without asking.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Like someone could go in to Tissue Land?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Tissue Land?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The land made of tissues.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Tissues, tissues, tissues.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I never heard of Tissue Land. All right, I'm going to turn off the lamp. Unless you want to?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You want to, Daddy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Boop. All right, there's the stars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Security Cats story?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: One.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's start from the Security Cats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ding dong, Security Cats!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Meow," said Wobbly Whiskers. "Who is it?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hi, Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And what did Seeker say? And what was Seeker's problem?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Oh, Security Cats, I'm glad you're here. 'Cause today I've got a big problem. You see, I use potions a lot for my magic research. You know about my potions?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And well, this morning I woke up and I looked in my potion closet, and they were all gone! Someone has stolen all of my potions!"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Even your potion ingredients and your potion tile?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, no. The ingredients are stored separately, and the potion tunnel is still there. But, the finished potions that I worked so hard on, they're all gone. What can we do, Security Cats?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Even the snake boot one?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Even the snake boot potion, that's right.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I know just the perfect way to get them back.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "You do?" said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: To take the underwater wave.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What do you mean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There's a potion I have in my pocket and it just takes me to the underwater wave. I slide down a tunnel first, and then I go under into the water wave.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Can I come with you?" said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So we're going underwater?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what are we going to do there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We're going to find... we ride the wave up to the shore, and then, and then, we got to find the potions.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then we collect the potions.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, how do you know they're at the shore?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Well, shouldn't we look for some clues, said Seeker?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm, where should we look first, do you think, Security Cats?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's let the Security Cats look for clues, because they're more better at searching for things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Wobbly Whiskers said, "All right, Seeker, where were you when you last saw your potions?" And Seeker said, "Well, normally they're in the potion closet. And so that's where I was when I saw them, if you know what I mean." And Wobbly Whiskers says, "I see, okay." And she was writing down in her little notepad about all the things she could find out about the mystery. And then she said, "And when did you last see them?" And Seeker said, "Last night."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And did anything unusual happen in your house last night or this morning? And Seeker said, "Well, one kind of weird thing did happen."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what was that, said Wobbly Whiskers? And Seeker said, "Well, I found a little flower on the floor in the potion closet that wasn't there before."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Wobbly Whiskers says, "What kind of flower?" And Seeker says, "I don't know."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. But maybe... Was it... Was all of it yellow on the top?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker says, "I don't remember. Let's go back to my castle and we can look around." And Seeker and Wobbly Whiskers and... the other Security Cat's name is...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sarana.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Sarana?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sarana.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Seeker and Wobbly and Sarana went back to Seeker's palace, to Seeker's castle I mean, and they looked... they opened up the door of the potion closet. And indeed all of the shelves were empty.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But on the floor was one tiny little flower.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did it have on it, which color was it?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was all yellow on top.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So it must be a dandelion.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a dandelion. But who would accidentally leave a dandelion here, do you think?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Wobbly Whiskers says, "Okay, we'll collect this as evidence." And she put it in a little baggie and wrote a label on it. And Seeker said, "Oh, I see, you're really doing detective work." And Sarana said, "I think we should look for any fingerprints or footprints or anything else in the dust." And Seeker said, "Yeah, the potion closet does get kind of dusty. It's hard to clean because of all the potions. But now you can see all the dust." And Sarana looked around, and did she see any interesting tracks or footprints?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, not quite yet. But she did see some weird dots. Some weird dots that ended by a small rock near the potion closet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, some weird dots.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And they were tiny, tiny, that you couldn't even see them, so Wobbly Whisker used a magnifying glass.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow! That's good detective work, said Seeker. What are these tiny dots, do you think?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. It's a trail.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's a trail. Here, let me look. And Seeker looked up very close with the magnifying glass, and he looked at one of the dots. And what shape was the dot?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A circle.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And were there any other tiny dots inside it or around it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It ended as a small rock.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So there was a trail of tiny dots that ended at a small rock.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Let's look even more closely to see if there's any clues. Are there any tiny hairs or fibers?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There's tiny hairs on them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What color hairs?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Gray hairs.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And it's someone gray.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And it's, and they're furry. Sounds like it had been my cat.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, and someone flew... And someone flew into Seeker's castle and said, "Hello, I'm Isabella. That was just me. That was just me."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "It was just you doing what?" said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was just my cat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Your cat? Your cat was here?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And my, my Silver. Silver was my, my pet cat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What was she doing here?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh. She just... I let her look at all the potions and she took all of them back to my house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She did! Oh my goodness, how did she carry all those potions?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I put them in a baggie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: To help her carry them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Why did you do that, said Seeker?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because Silver really wanted to bring every one home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: To test them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Seeker said that...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So it was just my cat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "That makes me kind of sad because I work really hard on these potions and I care about them a lot. And so if someone wants to borrow them, I like them to ask me first, and I can say if it's okay. But Silver took all these potions without asking. So I'm a little bit upset," said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What can we do about that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then someone other flew in.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's Dimitar.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hi, Dimitar. "I'm having kind of a rough day," said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, what's the matter, Seeker?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, you know Silver Cat? She took all my potions last night without asking. And she put them in a bag and brought them to Isabella's house, and I'm kind of mad about it. What do you think we should do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We thought we could return them when we were done seeing where they took us. But Silver wanted to keep them all for herself.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wait, Dimitar, you're saying you helped with this?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Dimitar didn't help. Isabella carried them for Silver. I carried them for Silver.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that was Isabella talking.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Dimitar, can you help me get all my potions back and figure out what to do?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What should we do first, Dimitar?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. Isabella's house is on Cornell and Roslindale.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Well, maybe we should go there. But we've only ever gone by taking a potion to get there. But now we don't have any potions, said Seeker, "I'm so mad! What are we going to do?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Seeker, you can take your sky ship.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, that's true.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You've got your sky ship.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's parked right outside. And so, Seeker and Dimitar and Wobbly Whiskers and Sarana all climbed up the tower where the sky ship was parked floating high above. And they climbed into the hatch inside the sky ship, and then they climbed up the stairs on the inside up onto the deck of the sky ship, and they looked out over the railing and they could see the whole countryside all around. Seeker's castle below them, and all the other creatures' houses, Hattie's workshop, and the ocean with the circular island in the distance where Dimitar lives.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Okay, how do we get to Isabella's house from here?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Farmer Wobbly, too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, the square island is out there, you can see it from here too, that's true, we're high enough.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And, and...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The woodchuck flew up and dropped some carrots in Seeker's lap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thanks Wobbly. I mean, thanks, um, Farmer Wobbly.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Farmer Wobbly said, "No problem. We've got a big crop of carrots this year, more than we can even eat. Hope you like them."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Well, I'll have a little snack, that'll help me feel better. 'Cause I woke up and I didn't have potions, and I didn't even want to eat breakfast because I was so mad. But now I'll have some carrots, chomp chomp chomp, and I'm starting to feel a little bit better and, oh yeah, I remember, to get to Isabella's house we got to fly the sky ship over all those mountains that way. We're going to see the Crystal Mountain on the way. But once we cross over the mountains, whoosh! And the sky ship flew straight up into the clouds and zoomed over the mountains. And then when they crossed over the mountains, they were in the land of Boston."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And crossed over...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Isabella lives in...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Where do you live?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Roslindale, which is part of Boston.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I live in Roslindale, remember?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yeah, said Seeker. And so he flew the ship over the city of Boston...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And, and, and Seeker already heard some meowing from inside Isabella's house. Meow, meow, meow, meow, meow, meow, meow, meow, meow, meow, meow, meow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Meow, meow, meow, meow, meow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "I think we should park the sky ship in the backyard. Should we park it on the patio, or in the grass, or maybe we should just tie it up on the swing set? What do you think?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, swing set. Or maybe we could park it in my driveway, 'cause my driveway's available.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ah, okay. And so...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It doesn't have my car in it right now. My mom's out shopping.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Great. So Seeker parked the sky ship on the driveway, and they followed the meows.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Meow, meow, meow, meow, meow, meow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And they saw Silver.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They saw Silver through the sunroom window meowing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And she was trying to get a fly.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Silver's trying to get a fly, Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow! And so Seeker and Dimitar and Isabella and Sarana and Wobbly Whiskers all went in the front door of Isabella's house and went over to the sunroom, and they saw Silver jumping in the air just as she caught a fly between her paws, and then chomp chomp, eat it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then she caught another.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow! And Seeker said, "Um, excuse me, Silver. I think you have something that belongs to me."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did Silver say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She said, "Meow."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Does anybody here speak cat? And Wobbly Whiskers says, "Why yes. Silver is saying she doesn't know what you mean. But I think she does. She has that kind of look in her eye of mischief."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Do you have my potions?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Definitely.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Meow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Wobbly Whiskers says, "She's saying no."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Let me talk to her. Meow meow meow meow..."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Wow, you just said a lot!" And then Silver said...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Meow, meow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did that mean?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Wobbly Whiskers says, "I just explained to her that you were feeling very upset because she had taken the potions without asking, and we know she has them. And so we're hoping that she will give them back."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Silver said, "Meow meow meow meow, meow meow meow, meow meow meow."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Wobbly Whiskers said, "Oh, that's very nice of you, Silver. Because she said where the potions were so that Seeker could find them. And where did Silver put the potions in Isabella's house?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: In Isabella's storage closet in her bedroom.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they all went up to look in Isabella's closet in her bedroom, and there they were, all the potions in a bag.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Wobbly Whiskers says, "And Seeker, Silver also says that she's going to make it up to you. She's going to help you feel better by doing something to help you, something very kind."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now that what I can give you, Seeker, one of my potions. Want to see one of my potions? I have a fire potion to take me to the fire... to the Fire Island, or a water potion to take me underwater, or a prehistoric land potion.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Those sound like wonderful potions, thank you. Which one of them do you want?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'll take the prehistoric potion.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here's the prehistoric potion.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you, that helps me feel better.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, Silver, I'm glad we worked things out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Silver said, "Meow meow meow meow meow."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Wobbly Whiskers says, "Yes, that means she's really sorry, and now she learned her lesson to ask before she takes things from people, even though she's a cat."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But she can take flies without asking.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, that's a different matter altogether, isn't it, said Seeker. And Seeker brought his potions back to his potion closet and arranged them neatly once again where they belonged on the shelves, including the brand new gift of a potion to the prehistoric world. And that's the end of Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Shadow puppets! Can you show me five of your shadows?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Five, that's a lot. Well, here's the number five. Five fingers on the ceiling.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Smaller, bigger, smaller, bigger. Woah. And that's one. And here's a funny shape. It's kind of a square. I can almost make a square. There. And here's a... almost like a... I can make a circle. It's not a very... it's kind of a potato.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Not, not every shape.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Here's a... a creature with three nasty teeth. Chomp, chomp, chomp. Chomp, chomp, chomp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And here is a person with a funny face. "Hello, I have a funny-shaped face, what do you think of that?" And one more.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'm a bunny. Here's my, here's my ears. I can wiggle them, wiggle, wiggle, wiggle, wiggle, wiggle. And that's my hand shadows for tonight.

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Wobbly Whiskers
characters.description: A small white kitten, slick with the sticky, iridescent residue of the potion bottle she tumbled from, who joins the adventure in a desperate search for her lost mother.

characters.name: Sarana
characters.description: The other Security Cat who assists in gathering clues and investigating the crime scene.

characters.name: Silver
characters.description: Immortalized in the stone of the Great Library yet reborn in Cat World as a small, hungry kitten, Izzy is a brave companion who now traverses the heart of Limbo. Communicating through an ancient, rhythmic language of resonant purrs and wielding a masterfully handled magical can opener, this tiny spirit balances her arcane legacy with a newfound, desperately urgent need for snuggles.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: Dimitar
characters.description: A sharp-witted resident of a circular island castle, this two-century-old purple dragonet carries a pirate’s salt-crusted legacy and a fierce devotion to his salvaged library. Formerly of the Cadasori galleries, Seeker’s grumpy companion now roams the deep to save his ancestral shores, though a recent volcanic disturbance has left him uncharacteristically sickened and frail. Driven by habit and a tireless appetite for fish, he bickers incessantly over snacks while guarding his remaining volumes with a brooding, protective intensity.

characters.name: Farmer Wobbly
characters.description: A woodchuck who lives on a square island and generously gifts Seeker carrots from his bountiful harvest.

characters.name: Hattie
characters.description: A clever mouse milliner and engineer of whimsy, she is a resourceful spirit who crafts exquisite hats and soulful portraits within her hollow tree stump workshop. Though a formidable presence famously counted among the thirty legendary cats, she is currently confined to her bed by illness, her usual treks through saturated woodlands for rare tape and lollipops temporarily halted. Even in her malaise, her sharp mind lingers on a recent, stinging disagreement with Dimitar over a fish, a rare shadow cast upon a heart otherwise known for its boundless generosity and creative light.

places.name: Tissue Land
places.description: A mythical, whimsical land made entirely of soft tissues.

places.name: Seeker's Castle
places.description: Behind an unassuming facade and a solitary doorbell lies Seeker’s home, a sprawling magical fortress that serves as a vital sanctuary for weary legends. The interior expands into a labyrinthine refuge where an expansive library of mystical books towers beside a potion closet that acts as the true gateway to adventure. This fortress is a hub for calls to action and well-earned rest, where Wobbly Whiskers waits to greet the Seeker and Buddy the Mouse—though the warmth of their homecoming is inevitably punctuated by his latest sticky mess upon the rug.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.

places.name: Circular Island
places.description: A perfectly circular landmass rising from the waters of the distant reaches, this remote sanctuary serves as both the private residence of Dimitar and the hallowed grounds of the school.

places.name: Square Island
places.description: A mysterious island where every single object and feature is shaped like a square.

places.name: Crystal Mountain
places.description: The caretaker’s secluded dwelling serves as a hallowed sanctuary where magical birds descend to harvest the luminous crystals essential for their flight.

places.name: Boston
places.description: The large city region where Isabella lives.

places.name: Roslindale
places.description: The specific neighborhood in Boston where Isabella's home is located.

places.name: Isabella's House
places.description: A home on Cornell Street with a basement containing bins of storybooks.
